issue_title: pipenv rewrite lock file badly when using git submodules
issue_body:
`pipenv, version 8.3.2`

Use Case:
- python project A uses submodules to include a submodule B in a directory `deps/B`
- dependency is registered in `Pipfile` with: `pipenv install -e ./deps/B`. Result is:

    B = {path = "./deps/B", editable = true}

`Pipfile.lock` is correctly updated to:

        "B": {
            "editable": true,
            "path": "./deps/B"
        },

So, it works great after this, the lock file is fine, the environment is fine.

However, next time `pipenv install` is executed (to install a new package), the `Pipfile.lock` is modified to:

        "B": {
            "editable": true,
            "git": "git@myserver:path/to/B.git@0c8fa9152a794312a1ca20a2b709d5043c327247",
            "subdirectory": "../../../deps/B"
        },

For information, this dependency uses [relative URL](https://docs.gitlab.com/ce/ci/git_submodules.html#configuring-the-gitmodules-file) from gitlab, but this should not impact pipenv. It's like when it generate the lock file the second time, it execute a different code where it discovers it is a git directory and try to add the url in the lock file. My recommendation is to provide a way for user to disable this "magic" git-awareness discovery and stick to what user entered when he did a `pipenv install ...`

Workaround: have to `pipenv --rm && pipenv install --dev` after each manual `pipenv install mynewpackage`

Thanks

Firstly thank you Kenneth for Pipenv and being able to meet you and the rest of the Pipenv team at PyCon this year. Trying to now use Pipenv in our daily workflow at work and have come into a few snags.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
At work all our systems are dockerised and both local development and deployments / production use the same docker image. We `docker run --rm <image> bash` locally and do our development from there. Our Dockerfile contains these build steps
```
RUN pip install pipenv
COPY Pipfile Pipfile.lock /tmp/
RUN cd /tmp && pipenv install --deploy --system --dev
...
```
The issue comes when we start developing inside the image and want to install new packages. As its a container we dont need any virtualenvs. There seems to be no way to install a single package straight to system. This has caused a bit of confusion as all the base packages are importable (since they where installed from a Pipfile with --system, but a new package installed while developing with `pipenv install x` is not importable (from system python) as it goes into a virtualenv.

This leads us to having to install these packages twice. `pipenv install x` (to get it into the Pipfile) and then `pipenv install --system` to then reinstall the Pipfile with the new package for system python to be able to see it.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be nice to have 1) --system when installing of specific packages and then 2) a way of setting --system globally so we can set it as an ENV in our dockerfile as Pipenv in docker would only ever be used with system Python. I feel this would be the use case for most people doing development inside docker containers.

I have looked into the code and noticed there are some undocumented envs that would turn on --system by default (but its not 100% clear and not a pipenv specific variable, seems like I need to 
`export VIRTUAL_ENV= `
for python to be able to cast it to False correctly https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/environments.py#L52). But as a found out it does not apply for installing specific packages. Just from the pipfile.

issue_title: do not re-lock dependencies that are already locked when doing a pipenv install
issue_body:
Do not re-lock dependencies that are already locked when doing a `pipenv install <package>`. If I have a lockfile and want to add a new package, there does not seem to be a sane way to add that package without triggering pipenv to upgrade the locked versions of all installed packages.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: `macos 10.13.3`
2. Python version: `Python 2.7.10`
3. Pipenv version: `pipenv, version 9.0.3`

##### Expected result

Run `pipenv install pkgA` and then later `pipenv install pkgB` and see that `pkgA` was untouched, still pinned where it was before.

##### Actual result

Run `pipenv install pkgA` and then later `pipenv install pkgB` and see that pkgA was upgraded to a newer version just because I installed unrelated `pkgB`.

In yarn this is very clearly separated by `yarn install` versus `yarn upgrade` where it will only re-lock the dependency if you change its pin range and run `yarn install` or if you run `yarn upgrade`. If you run `yarn install` without changing the pin on the dependency then it is never upgraded.

issue_title: Remove test command and tests_require in favor of tox
issue_body:
In [this comment](/pypa/setuptools/issues/917#issuecomment-274325745), @graingert proposes that we may be able to completely remove support for tests_require instead of transitioning that tooling from `easy_install` to `pip install`.

While he didn't directly propose removal, the effort would only benefit that ticket if the install functionality were completely removed, so let's explore what that will entail.

While I agree that `tox` is an excellent, powerful, robust solution, it's more heavy than `tests_require`, requiring that the user have tox installed in advance. As a more thorough solution, it also is subject to bugs and constraints that a simpler test runner is not. There are several advantages to `setup.py test` and `tests_require` over tox:

- Doesn't have any prerequisites (except the implicit one on Setuptools, which is generally assumed).
- As a result of not having any prerequisites, invocation is often a simple invocation (`setup.py test`) instead of multiple steps (e.g. `pip install --user tox; python -m tox`).
- `setup.py test` allows for invocation under a number of different Python versions naturally (i.e. `python3.3 setup.py test` or `py -3.3 setup.py test`) whereas tox offers "run under the python in which tox is installed" or "run for explicitly-declared python versions".
- As `setup.py test` doesn't use virtualenv, it's not subject to virtualenv bugs or other constraints imposed by virtualenv (such as version 14 dropping support for Python 3.2.).
- As `setup.py test` doesn't rely on pip, it's not subject to the bugs of pip (such as issues with `--editable` installs or namespace packages) or other constraints imposed by pip (such as dropping support for Python 3.2).

I consider these advantages small and easy enough to overcome, especially now that many of these issues have been resolved in setuptools, pip, and virtualenv. If we can get to a place that tox can broadly supplant the uses cases of setup.py test and [pytest-runner](https://pypi.org/project/pytest-runner) (and thus tests_require) in practice, then yes, deprecating and removing it would be in order. Given the amount of activity and bugs I see around these tools, I'd asses they're still in active use.

Before flagging this functionality as deprecated, I'd like to survey the community about the possibility to see if there are use cases that would prove difficult to support with tox.

@graingert, would you be interested in being the champion for this effort (removing tests_require), starting with the outreach on [distutils-sig](https://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/distutils-sig) and then implementing the deprecation/removal changes?

The [current CCS 4 draft](https://drafts.csswg.org/selectors/#the-nth-child-pseudo) has `:nth-child(An+B [of S]? )`, extending `:nth-child(An+B)`

> The :nth-child(An+B [of S]? ) pseudo-class notation represents the An+Bth element that matches the selector list S among its inclusive siblings.
> The CSS Syntax Module [CSS3SYN] defines the An+B notation. If S is omitted, it defaults to *.

> By passing a selector argument, we can select the Nth element that matches that selector. For example, the following selector matches the first three “important” list items, denoted by the .important class:
> `:nth-child(-n+3 of li.important)`

issue_title: FireWorks de-serializes even PyTask input objects that are themselves JSON-serialized
issue_body:
Consider this situation:

a user is using the PyTask to carry out an operation, and as a part of that, transfers a python object that has been serialized in JSON format (with json or jsonpickle) as an input to that PyTask. Then (it seems, based on my own testing), upon starting the job, Fireworks will de-serialize also the input object to the PyTask, resulting in errors. Perhaps Fireworks should make an effort to not alter the format of inputs to PyTasks?

Actually, after looking at my problem more, it doesn't seem like this is correct, and I am likely doing something else wrong.

issue_title: Inconsistent 2D vs 3D interpolation behavior for mode 'reflect'
issue_body:
Currently, interpolation gives different results than one would expect in the 3D case for mode reflect.  Likely this is a problem in ``ndi.map_coordinates`` rather than scikit-image itself, but a potential workaround I propose below is to call map_coordinates with mode 'mirror' instead of reflect. 


Here is the output of ``test_resize2d`` from skimage/transforms/tests/test_warps.py if I change the mode from 'constant' to 'reflect'.  This tests still passes and looks as expected:
![reflect_2d](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6528957/8573443/9395e116-255f-11e5-9b38-6dd2b1a3aacd.png)



However, for ``test_resize3d_resize``, the output is not as expected, and the test fails:
![reflect_3d](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6528957/8573402/502df7f6-255f-11e5-98ca-9523377857d5.png)


If I change the mode passed to ndi.map_coordinates to 'mirror', rather than 'reflect', the expected result is restored.  
![mirror_3d](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6528957/8573467/b44e5744-255f-11e5-8807-c65584dd5710.png)

The code producing the above figures is as follows:
```python
import numpy as np
from skimage.transform import resize
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt


""" 2D reflect test """

# copied from test_resize2d()
x = np.zeros((5, 5), dtype=np.double)
x[1, 1] = 1
resized = resize(x, (10, 10), order=0, mode='reflect')
ref = np.zeros((10, 10))
ref[2:4, 2:4] = 1

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3)
axes[0].imshow(x, interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[0].set_axis_off()
axes[0].set_title("original")
axes[1].imshow(resized, interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[1].set_axis_off()
axes[1].set_title("resized")
axes[2].imshow(ref, interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[2].set_axis_off()
axes[2].set_title("expected result")


""" 3D reflect test """

# copied from test_resize3d_resize()
x = np.zeros((5, 5, 3), dtype=np.double)
x[1, 1, :] = 1
resized = resize(x, (10, 10, 1), order=0, mode='reflect')
ref = np.zeros((10, 10, 1))
ref[2:4, 2:4] = 1

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3)
axes[0].imshow(x[:, :, 1], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[0].set_axis_off()
axes[0].set_title("original[:, :, 1]")
axes[1].imshow(resized[:, :, 0], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[1].set_axis_off()
axes[1].set_title("resized")
axes[2].imshow(ref[:, :, 0], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[2].set_axis_off()
axes[2].set_title("expected result")


""" 3D mirror test """

# copied from test_resize3d_resize()
x = np.zeros((5, 5, 3), dtype=np.double)
x[1, 1, :] = 1
resized = resize(x, (10, 10, 1), order=0, mode='mirror')
ref = np.zeros((10, 10, 1))
ref[2:4, 2:4] = 1

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3)
axes[0].imshow(x[:, :, 1], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[0].set_axis_off()
axes[0].set_title("original[:, :, 1]")
axes[1].imshow(resized[:, :, 0], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[1].set_axis_off()
axes[1].set_title("resized")
axes[2].imshow(ref[:, :, 0], interpolation='nearest', cmap=plt.cm.gray)
axes[2].set_axis_off()
axes[2].set_title("expected result")

```

I just tried using `peak_local_max` and was puzzled to find no peaks (on a small test image). 
After reading the code I realized it was because the default `min_distance` of 10 (for minimum peak--peak separation) is also used as the border width of the `exclude_border` functionality. 

I find this behaviour bizarre frankly; usually exclude border for this type of algorithm means exclude border pixels, not a larger width (which is tied to a separate functionality!). 

I would suggest one of the following changes: 

* Either set the border width to be 1 pixel when `exclude_border` is `True`
* Or change `exclude_border` to be a border width (possible renaming?) such that e.g. `exclude_border=0` includes the border, while e.g. `exclude_border=5` excludes a border region of 5 pixels.

issue_title: Remove IO package in favor of imageio
issue_body:
This is intended for `scikit-image` v1.0:

- Replace `PIL` dependency with `imageio`
- Import all of `imageio` API into `skimage.io` namespace.
- Move `ImageCollection` to `imageio`
- Keep image stack
- Remove `external.tifffile`
- Use `matplotlib` only for `show`.

I have added plugins to `imageio` for `tifffile`, `gdal`, `fits`, and `itk`.

`imageio` only depends on `libfreeimage`, which is included in Debian repos, and will be included within the wheel installs for OSX and Windows.  There is a cross-platform `conda` recipe for `libfreeimage`.

Benefits:
- No `PIL`!
- Plugins are selected based on file extension, but can be overridden.
- A sane API for multi-image read/write.
- We benefit from any additional plugins that are added.

here is the test code example:

```
 import numpy as np

issue_title: Improve docs for description.py and prediction output (for hotgym and in general)
issue_body:
From the [mailing list](http://lists.numenta.org/pipermail/nupic_lists.numenta.org/2014-January/002524.html):

I'm executing the examples that come with nupic installation and was wondering if there is some documentation that explain better the description.py files and the inference output files. 

For example, how should i interpret all the columns of the CSV output file and the last few lines of the description file of hotgym example?

I had some clues from here https://github.com/numenta/nupic/wiki/Spatial-Classification and here https://github.com/numenta/nupic/wiki/Online-Prediction-Framework but it isn't enough. 

    #hotgym description.py

    # Logged Metrics: A sequence of regular expressions that specify which of
    # the metrics from the Inference Specifications section MUST be logged for
    # every prediction. The regex's correspond to the automatically generated
    # metric labels. This is similar to the way the optimization metric is
    # specified in permutations.py.
      'loggedMetrics': ['.*aae.*'],

    # Add multi-step prediction metrics
    for steps in config['predictionSteps']:
      control['metrics'].append(    
          MetricSpec(field=config['predictedField'], metric='multiStep', 
                     inferenceElement='multiStepBestPredictions', 
                     params={'errorMetric': 'aae', 'window': 1000, 'steps': steps}))
      control['metrics'].append(    
          MetricSpec(field=config['predictedField'], metric='trivial', 
                     inferenceElement='prediction', 
                     params={'errorMetric': 'aae', 'window': 1000, 'steps': steps}))
      control['metrics'].append(    
          MetricSpec(field=config['predictedField'], metric='multiStep', 
                     inferenceElement='multiStepBestPredictions', 
                     params={'errorMetric': 'altMAPE', 'window': 1000, 'steps': steps}))
      control['metrics'].append(    
          MetricSpec(field=config['predictedField'], metric='trivial', 
                     inferenceElement='prediction', 
                     params={'errorMetric': 'altMAPE', 'window': 1000, 'steps': steps}))

Relates to #548.

pip install --upgrade nupic

Currently nupic has 2 types of temporal processors: TemporalPooler and TemporalMemory. Both are implemented in "pure python" and "hibrid python with C++" versions. However their names are not suggestive enough to someone correlates such implementations. I suggest we rename TemporalPoolers tastes to correspond to TemporalMemory name. In this case:
- `TP` (pure python TemporalPooler) become `temporal_pooler/TemporalPooler` to correlate to `temporal_memory.py/TemporalMemory`
- `TP10x2` (hybrid python TemporalPooler) become `fast_temporal_pooler.py/FastTemporalPooler` to correlate to `fast_temporal_memory.py/FastTemporalMemory`

To reach SBP version 1.0, we should promote the file_io messages to stable:

MSG_FILEIO_READ
MSG_FILEIO_READ_DIR
MSG_FILEIO_REMOVE
MSG_FILEIO_WRITE

Insure their form and fitness for stability. In particular, remove bi-direction from these message. Presently,

FILEIO_READ
FILEIO_READ_DIR
FILEIO_WRITE

FILEIO_READ_DEVICE
FILEIO_READ_HOST
FILEIO_READ_DIR_DEVICE
FILEIO_READ_DIR_HOST

FILEIO_WRITE_HOST

issue_body: 'make html' uses some information from source tree and some from the installed sbp package

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

does not exist.

Hi

We are currently using the SBP library 2.2.15 for our [ethz_piksi_ros](https://github.com/ethz-asl/ethz_piksi_ros/tree/8d7d4caa03c4c383ecca5561001c40a287bf21c6) interface. There are two features we believe are missing in this current library version:

1.  A UART status message: for debugging it is crucial that we know the UART state, i.e., we want to know how many messages are sent / received.
2. We want to always have SPP and RTK information available. Currently, if Piksi is in a RTK Float or RTK Fix mode, no SPP messages are broadcasted. However, we want to always have SPP information and RTK on top of that, once (float or fix are) available.

We hope, that these features will be implemented into the library, since at least feature "2" should be very feasible, as SPP information is available at all times anyway. Also, both of these features were already available with Piksi V2.

Thanks in advance
Kai

issue_body:
**Problem:**
In ext.py we have this code:
```
A       @blueprint.record_once
        def extend_default_endpoint_prefixes(_):
             ...
B            current_records_rest = app.extensions['invenio-records-rest']
             ...

C       app.register_blueprint(blueprint)
```
`c` calls `A` right away. `B` depends on `invenio-records-rest` being already loaded.

**Solution:**
We cannot load the blueprint out of ext.py as `create_blueprint` needs the config.
Thus the simplest fix for now is to use `@app.before_first_request`.

**Problem with this solution:**
This however means that one cannot use `default_endpoint_prefixes` before the first request.

As ``RecordID`` PID provider is no longer planned in ``next`` branch of ``invenio-records``:

(master): https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records/blob/master/invenio_records/providers/recid.py

There should be one extra PID provider defined in the ``invenio-pidstore`` which depends on ``invenio_records``, similarly to how ``DataCite`` PID provider depends on the ``datacite`` package.

When loading the demo records there is an import error in the celery task worker:
`ImportError("import_string() failed for 'invenio_pidstore.providers.datacite.DataCite'.`

The problem can be fixed with installing the extra requirement `datacite`.

issue_title: Two or one digit number format of time results in different time zones
issue_body:
Hi,
we observed an odd behaviour of time string parsing

Example:
```python
    from DateTime import DateTime
    t1 = DateTime('2016-04-21 13:05')
    t2 = DateTime('2016-04-21 13:5')
    assert (t1 == t2), '%s is not equal to %s' % (t1, t2)
```
results in
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<console>", line 1, in <module>
AssertionError: 2016/04/21 13:05:00 GMT+0 is not equal to 2016/04/21 13:05:00 GMT+2

In summary, one digit number for hour, minute or second always returns TZ GMT+0. Two digit number format of time (hh:mm:ss) returns local TZ. Is this a bug or feature?
See zopefoundation/z3c.pt#9
I'll try to get around to this in the near-ish future.
Other zope packages create coverage statistics and publish them using coveralls.io. This would be nice here, too.

issue_title: Completely remove prices argument from alphalens.tears.* functions
issue_body:
While the main Alphalens functions work with forward returns, there are still the event study related functions that work with prices (create_event_study_tear_sheet and create_event_returns_tear_sheet).

It would be nice to have a  alphalens.utils.something  function similar to get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns that helps building the event study functions input from the prices. 

The formatted data returned by get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns looks like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15273575/28611208-51bf1d40-71eb-11e7-9049-a661a15a4009.png)

This format would fit the event study functions and the only change required would be to add a forward return column for each period specified in the event study range.
E.g. if `avgretplot=(5, 15)` we need to add forward return columns: -5, -4, -3, -2, ... , 12, 13, 14, 15

Unfortunately the memory usage for such data format would be too much. e.g.  universe of 1500 securities, 
for 15 years (equivalent to Quantopian Q1500US universe along the full date range avaliable on the platform) using `avgretplot=(5, 15)`
1500 (securities) * 252 (trading days in a year) * 15 (number of years) * 20 (avgretplot=(5, 15) * 8 (float64) =~ 1GB of Memory

I believe that's way too much memory, so I suggest we use another data format for event study related functions.

issue_title: Sentry captures sent via signal handler don't group

We get some raven captures out of signal handlers, e.g.

This is likely caused by OOM, but because the app-level stack is different on each of the OOM occurrences, the captures don't group.  

I think it would be good to have a convenience method around capture-from-signal-handler which clues raven into disregarding the stack for grouping purposes.  

I'm not clueful enough about raven-python internals to easily implement this -- could you give me a pointer about what `.capture()` params I should manipulate in the convenience wrapper?

If `apply()` were replaced by `__call__()` in
https://github.com/zalando/expan/blob/dev/expan/core/binning.py#L48

it would be easier to apply bins to data frames by simply writing `binned_df = bin(df, feature)`

`Experiment.delta()` is now a dispatcher, however those lines https://github.com/zalando/expan/blob/dev/expan/core/experiment.py#L368 duplicate the code https://github.com/zalando/expan/blob/dev/expan/core/experiment.py#L455

Additional complication: `delta()` function calls the other one, i.e. `fixed_horizon_delta()`

1. Do we really need classes for that? Do we ever use inheritance from the base `Binning` class? I've got a feeling that a bunch of functions would be enough for all our purposes.

2. Labelling: Instead of passing `format_str` to `Binning.labels`, user should pass a function that would return the appropriate label for given category, smth like
`labels = bins.labels(x, lambda i: 'label " + str(i))` thus a) getting rid of not very flexible labelling code and b) giving the user the flexibility of choosing any labelling scheme.

issue_title: Cannot get all attachments if several attachments have the same name
issue_body:
Hi there,

I was trying to retrieve some attachments from my test cases in Rally and some of them got several attachments with the same name. As the current function `getAttachments` is looping though attachments by names, this did not work. Moreover an error occured while trying to do it.

The current code:

    def getAttachments(self, artifact):
        """
            For the given Artifact, return a list of Attachment records.
            Each Attachment record will look like a Rally WSAPI Attachment with
            the additional Content attribute that will contain the decoded AttachmentContent.
        """
        attachment_names = self.getAttachmentNames(artifact)
        attachments = [self.getAttachment(artifact, attachment_name) for attachment_name in attachment_names]
        attachments = [att for att in attachments if att is not None]
        return attachments


I ended up writing my own function (that returns a different result though to fit the code where it is used) inspired by `getAttachements` and `getAttachment`

	import base64
	from pyral.rallyresp import RallyRESTResponse
        def getAttachments(self):
		'''
		  Get a list of  all the attachments for the test case 
	          (dictionary with 3 keys: 'Name', 'oid', and 'Content')
		'''
		listAttachments = list()
		tcAttachments = self.entity.Attachments
		#List
		for att in tcAttachments:
			if not att._hydrated:
				getattr(att, 'Description')  # forces the hydration to occur
				
			context, augments = self.rally.contextHelper.identifyContext()
			resp = self.rally._getResourceByOID(context, 'AttachmentContent', att.Content.oid, project=None)
			if resp.status_code not in [200, 201, 202]:
				return None

			response = RallyRESTResponse(self.rally.session, context, "AttachmentContent.x", resp, "full", 1)
			if response.errors or response.resultCount != 1:
				return None
			att_content = response.next()
			att.Content = base64.decodestring(att_content.Content)  # maybe further txfm to Unicode ?
		
			listAttachments.append({k:getattr(att,k) for k in ['Name','oid','Content']})
		return listAttachments

issue_title: StackDriver: ResourceExhausted: 429 Received message larger than max (5595329 vs. 4194304)
issue_body:
Hi

When trying to query Stackdriver metrics I am getting the following error `ResourceExhausted: 429 Received message larger than max`.
I feel like the max rpc call should be far over 4Mb, or the client should be to assemble multiple messages together.

Code example : 
```python
from google.cloud import monitoring_v3
from google.cloud.monitoring import enums
from google.cloud.monitoring_v3 import query

client = monitoring_v3.MetricServiceClient()

issue_title: sys.modules unpatching more trouble than it's worth
issue_body:
pex/pex.py has the following gem:

```python
  # Thar be dragons -- when this contextmanager exits, the interpreter is
  # potentially in a wonky state since the patches here (minimum_sys_modules
  # for example) actually mutate global state.  This should not be
  # considered a reversible operation despite being a contextmanager.
  @classmethod
  @contextmanager
  def patch_sys(cls):
    """Patch sys with all site scrubbed."""
    def patch_dict(old_value, new_value):
      old_value.clear()
      old_value.update(new_value)

    def patch_all(path, path_importer_cache, modules):
      sys.path[:] = path
      patch_dict(sys.path_importer_cache, path_importer_cache)
      patch_dict(sys.modules, modules)

    old_sys_path, old_sys_path_importer_cache, old_sys_modules = (
        sys.path[:], sys.path_importer_cache.copy(), sys.modules.copy())
    new_sys_path, new_sys_path_importer_cache, new_sys_modules = cls.minimum_sys()

    patch_all(new_sys_path, new_sys_path_importer_cache, new_sys_modules)

    try:
      yield   
    finally:
      patch_all(old_sys_path, old_sys_path_importer_cache, old_sys_modules)
```

The first patch_all replaces the global sys.path, sys.path_importer_cache and sys.modules.  This is fine, but the second patch_all replaces sys.modules with the original set, and thus de-refcounting all the modules that were imported during the lifecycle of the PEX.  If you've registered ``sys.excepthook``, ``atexit`` handlers or ``__del__``, chances are you've seen inscrutable exceptions like ``'NoneType' object has no attribute 'TBinaryProtocolAccelerated'`` or something.  This is because of CPython interpreter teardown behavior: https://bugs.python.org/issue18214.  This has since been "fixed" in CPython 3.4 but we can't mandate its use for everyone.

I'm proposing that we get rid of the unpatch.  It is only used during PEX.execute, which behaves like "exec()" and should be the last call in any sensible program.  I can't think of any reason to keep it other than some misguided sense of functional purity.

```

Note the "tar=TGZ"

`bundle.jvm` currently does not expose a product_type for any _archives_ it creates (only the loose bundle directory). And `binary.python-binary-create` currently does not expose any products at all. We would like these tasks to expose products, in order to be able to consume those products in downstream (internal) tasks.

In particular, JVM apps (at least at Twitter) are generally published as archived (zip'd/tar'd) bundles. And python apps are deployed directly as pexes.

issue_title: JarPublish should have the option to restrict push origins
issue_body:
Today JarPublish allow for restriction of push branches, but not - in git terminology - origin.  This can lead to a publish from any master even if it is not associated with the 'golden' origin.  This bit @ericzundel when publishing zinc from pantsbuild/pants recently.

Ideally, a 'golden' origin could be configured and JarPublish would use that.  Alternatively, it could check 'origin' against a list of allowed values and fail the publish if there is no match.

pypi now takes a while to make some packages available after publishing -- at this exact moment, [`pantsbuild.pants.contrib.buildgen`](https://pypi.org/project/pantsbuild.pants.contrib.buildgen/1.9.0rc0/) is apparently up, but cannot be resolved when I run `./build-support/bin/release.sh -t`.

One method of making this easier is to make the release script hit e.g. https://pypi.python.org/simple/pantsbuild-pants-contrib-buildgen/ and with some form of backoff until `1.9.0rc0` is up, when testing each package.

Alternatively, it would be good to be able to test resolves more immediately -- @kwlzn mentioned on slack that this may be worth a ticket against pypi. I am not sure exactly what this ticket would entail yet.

issue_title: Replacing cell text and keeping existing style and alignment
issue_body:
Let me start by saying GREAT LIBRARY!! Here's the problem. I'm opening an existing document and need to replace the values in a couple of cells in a table. The original value of the cell may be empty, but the cell has a style attached and is formatted for a CENTER alignment. Here is the row from opc output as it comes to me in the original docx. 1.0 Initial version I then run the following code 
revTableCell = doc.tables[0].cell(4,0)
revTableCell.text = u'2015-08-04'

revTableCell = doc.tables[0].cell(4,1)
tpar = revTableCell.add_paragraph(u'New Version',style='a_Table_Text')
tpar.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER

revTableCell = doc.tables[0].cell(4,2)
revTableCell.text = u''
tpar = revTableCell.add_paragraph(u'New Author',style='a_Table_Text')
tpar.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER`
and I end up with the following in the docx. 2015-08-04 1.0 New Version New Author Initial version In the first cell I write a new text string to the cell using _Cell.text = 'string'. It appears that the style is completely removed although the alignment is kept. That makes sense. In the second cell I run _Cell.add_paragraph() so I can apply a style. This doesn't get rid of the original string. In the third cell, I attempt both a text assignment and an add_paragraph. What you get is two runs. (Again makes sense). It doesn't appear to me that the 'paragraph_format' property is available from the _Cell object. If it is available an example of how to reference it would be helpful. Is there a way to simply replace the text of a cell without disturbing the existing formatting and style? Thank you. Kevin

issue_title: downloadURL missing for plain uploaded files
issue_body:
Seems PyDrive relies on the **downloadUrl** meta property on files but that property has been removed in Google Drive API v3.

Doing .GetContentFile() raises "_pydrive.files.FileNotDownloadableError: No downloadLink/exportLinks for mimetype found in metadata_"

However, the file metadata does come with **webContentLink** property which seems to work the same way at least for plain uploaded drive files.

I was able to get around the issue by doing:

```
  file_obj.FetchMetadata(fetch_all=True)
  file_obj.metadata['downloadUrl'] = file_obj.metadata.get('webContentLink')
  file_obj.GetContentFile('/tmp/test.csv')
```

..but obviously that's a hack.  

Any guidance on cleaner way to solve this?

Thanks
Pete

issue_title: play_from_queue always makes call to self.avTransport.SetAVTransportURI
issue_body:
The play_from_queue function in soco.core always makes a call to self.avTransport.SetAVTransportURI. As the comment in the python code explains, this is to set the queue as the source URI on the device.

In some circumstances this may be a necessary step (e.g. if the device has been set to play from line-in), but they seldom apply. 

The downside of this approach is that every time a user wishes to jump to another track in the queue, the queue is first reinstated on the device. This is an expensive operation which triggers additional events and causes delay. In an application which is monitoring events to keep the user interface updated, these additional events are undesirable. 

For example, there will be an unnecessary transitional event that the queue position has jumped to the first track in the queue. A user interface monitoring these events will jump to the first track before jumping to the track being set by the call to play_from_queue.

As a result, I'm no longer using this soco function, but rather making a direct call to self.avTransport.Seek. This avoids the unnecessary overhead of reinstating the queue on the device.

This solves my problem, but I wonder if soco.core might be updated to address the concern. Could a check be made to see if the queue is already on the device before reloading it, so that reloading occurs only if the queue is not loaded? Or could there be another soco.core function (jump_to_position) which only calls self.avTransport.Seek to set the position? This would be consistent with the previous and next functions, which don't reinstate the queue.

issue_title: Option to use e-mail field value in 'reply-to'
issue_body:
Many email gateway providers (including mandril and sparkpost) don't accept e-mail from unverified domains. 

Therefore notification emails should not be sent with the sender being the e-mail field value. The sender address should be configurable (as it currently is through Aldryn Email Settings or settings.py). This email address can then be verified with the email gateway provider.

However to allow for easy responding in email clients, a reply-to header should be settable to the submitted email fields value.

issue_title: The `collection.items` method does not obey the `offset` parameter
issue_body:
From a discussion on the Community Slack channel. This function call:

```
client.threads.where(in_='inbox', limit=thread_size, offset=mail_offset).items()
```

Does not behave as expected. `items` ignores the provided offset and always returns starting with the 0th item. (https://github.com/nylas/nylas-python/blob/master/nylas/client/restful_model_collection.py#L22).

Alternatively, there's a method called `all`, but confusingly it obeys the preset offset but requires that you pass the limit as a parameter, ignoring the limit set in the where clause. This is also reported as not working as expected.

`client.threads.where(in_='inbox', offset=mail_offset).all(limit=thread_size)`

Ideally `items` and `all`would be merged together, and would obey the limit and offset provided in a where clause.
Setting up Confidant should be simplified, perhaps with a configuration management script that creates the correct KMS and IAM resources.

yields the error

`confidant: error: unrecognized arguments: -u https://confidant-production.example.com -k alias/authnz-production --from myservice-production --to confidant-production --user_type service --region us-west-2`

It should yield a url unreachable or kms auth error, not an argument error if the example is correct.

Besides automating testing, there is one other thing that needs doing to get this ready for a 1.0.0: whipping the codebase into shape to following established coding guidelines. PEP-8 is the standard to go by; we should follow it (and then enforce it with a `flake8` test).

Yes, I realize this will result in one set of "janitor" patches that are really poorly liked, and deservedly so. But I still maintain it's the best way forward to fix things once, and then stick to the standard.

2017-06-09 08:05:40 INFO install   File "/usr/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.p

Websocket connection error 1009 shows:

> The max_size parameter enforces the maximum size for incoming messages in bytes. The default value is 1MB. None disables the limit. If a message larger than the maximum size is received, recv() will raise ConnectionClosed and the connection will be closed with status code 1009.

Changing juju/client/connection.py:
self.ws = await websockets.connect(url, **kw)
to
self.ws = await websockets.connect(url, max_size=None, **kw)

Fixes the connection issues.

If I repeatedly call reduce or marginalize on the same tabular CPD using different variables I get an error. issue_title: Issues with interpolation based on generated .designspace
issue_body: Here is a nice one! It could somehow be related to #264 but I don’t think so. We have a single-axis font with three masters, two in extremes, one in the middle. Fontmake .glyphs --> .ufo + .designspace works fine. If I then interpolate instances using the generated .designspace, bad things happen. More specifically, it seems that the y-direction is interpolated differently from the x-direction. And the cool thing, if I remove the `` part from the .designspace doc, everything is interpolated correctly. The screenshot shows the correct outline in the background, the interpolated in the front. issue_title: STAT tables for families which consist of more than two variable fonts
issue_body:
I've attempted writing this issue three times so I'm writing up a common scenario I encounter.

Many families hosted on Google Fonts have been drawn using two glyphs sources e.g [Nunito](https://github.com/googlefonts/nunito). One for Roman styles the other for Italic styles. Since both sources are not MM compatible, we'll end up with two variable fonts. Since Fontmake will generate from each glyphs source independently, neither font will reference the other font in their STAT table. We ideally want STAT tables which are similar to the example given in [MS STAT table spec example 5](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/stat#example-5-a-family-comprised-of-a-non-italic-variable-font-plus-an-italic-variable-font). 

I understand that fonttools' varLib is only able to produce a basic STAT table at the moment. However, when it can generate better STAT tables, I feel fontmake should be aware of all the fonts that exist for the given family and produce STAT tables based on this knowledge.

I find this table rather hard to follow. The spec insists that all variable fonts need this table which is fine. However, are the tables which fontmake currently generates good enough? what are the implications if we ship families which consist of more than two fonts and their stat tables don't reference each other?

Please close this if you feel it isn't relevant Fontmake. I've read the other STAT issues and feel this warrants its own discussion.

issue_title: OSError: 'File name too long' when caching AWS S3 files
issue_body:
The [allennlp-as-a-library-example](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp-as-a-library-example) downloads its example data and models from an AWS S3 server and caches them on the filesystem, but I'm getting the following error:

```
0it [00:00, ?it/s]2018-05-15 16:04:48,626 - INFO - allennlp.common.file_utils - https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/allennlp/datasets/academic-papers-example/train.jsonl not found in cache, downloading to /tmp/tmphvj02xzn
                                                               2018-05-15 16:04:55,187 - INFO - allennlp.common.file_utils - copying /tmp/tmphvj02xzn to cache at /home/goodmami/.allennlp/datasets/aHR0cHM6Ly9zMy11cy13ZXN0LTIuYW1hem9uYXdzLmNvbS9hbGxlbm5scC9kYXRhc2V0cy9hY2FkZW1pYy1wYXBlcnMtZXhhbXBsZS90cmFpbi5qc29ubA==.dfeac5182442931a79450b22cef3816b-7
Traceback (most recent call last):
  [...omitted...]
  File "/home/goodmami/env/allennlp/lib/python3.6/site-packages/allennlp/common/file_utils.py", line 125, in get_from_cache
    shutil.copyfile(temp_filename, cache_path)
  File "/home/goodmami/env/allennlp/lib/python3.6/shutil.py", line 121, in copyfile
    with open(dst, 'wb') as fdst:
OSError: [Errno 36] File name too long: '/home/goodmami/.allennlp/datasets/aHR0cHM6Ly9zMy11cy13ZXN0LTIuYW1hem9uYXdzLmNvbS9hbGxlbm5scC9kYXRhc2V0cy9hY2FkZW1pYy1wYXBlcnMtZXhhbXBsZS90cmFpbi5qc29ubA==.dfeac5182442931a79450b22cef3816b-7'
```

I'm using AllenNLP 0.4.2, installed via Pip, on Pop!_OS 17.10 (an Ubuntu derivative).

The method of caching files using a base64-encoded version of their remote URL (see [allennlp.common.file_utils](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/b71ef434b310e9b1ba400b42c7334c794996b40e/allennlp/common/file_utils.py))  is clever but not scalable as URL length limits (usually ~2000 characters) are much larger than file name lengths (usually no more than 255 characters (see [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems#Limits)). Generally this issue won't come up, as a URL such as `https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/allennlp/datasets/academic-papers-example/train.jsonl` (88 characters) is 155 characters when base64-encoded. However, if a user encrypts their home directory with [ecryptfs](http://ecryptfs.org/), the maximum file name length is reduced to 143 characters (see [here](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ecryptfs/+bug/344878) and [here](https://unix.stackexchange.com/a/32834/258365)), which leads to the problem shown above. Encryptfs is a file-based encryption system, and I don't think that full-disk encryption (e.g., with LUKS) suffers the same limitation, but encryptfs is a widely used implementation and is enabled by default on some Linux distributions. If it wasn't clear above, note that the issue *can* affect users who don't use encryptfs, but it requires URLs about 2--3x longer to hit the 255-character limit.

Some possible solutions:
* Break up the URL and base64-encoding each part such that each non-final part is a directory (e.g., `b64encode("https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com")` becomes a directory containing `b64encode("/allennlp/datasets/academic-papers-example/train.jsonl")`). This solution only mitigates the issue a bit, but doesn't solve it.
* Store the files in a non-encrypted part of the filesystem, e.g., `/opt/allennlp-data/...`, but this is a terrible solution: it introduces permissions issues and doesn't change the 255 character limit, and the user may actually want the data encrypted.
* Use an index to map URLs to some new filename (e.g., using Python's `tempfile` module)

I think the last solution is best. Since you already have a directory for storing the cached files, it should be painless to add a `index.json` file or something.

issue_title: unbounded metric_queue can lead to memory exhaustion
issue_body:
I was tracking down a memory usage issue with the `SyncManager` process tonight.
After a good look at the code, it seems like under the right circumstances  `SyncManager` can eat up all available memory.

The root of the issue is that the `metric_queue`, that is shared by all collectors, is unbounded.
If, for whatever reason,  the handlers are not popping stuff fast enough from that queue, the `SyncManager` process (which owns the queue) will grow until it reaches the system limit (or gets killed by the OOM killer)

We've encountered this issue with a single graphite handler that was configured with a wrong host,  the host was unreachable and every connection attempt would timeout  (and the default graphite timeout is 15 seconds, that doesn't help either).
We had ~10 collectors running and configured to poll every 10s. After a few hours, the `SyncManager` was _huge_  ;-)

I'm wondering what would be the right approach to fix this, or even if one approach could fit all needs.

In my case, I'd rather lose some metrics than have a process eat up all available memory (before it gets killed... we'd lose the stats anyways).

For this we could add a  (configurable?) maximum queue size:
```
--- a/src/diamond/server.py
+++ b/src/diamond/server.py
@@ -58,7 +58,7 @@ class Server(object):
         self.manager = multiprocessing.Manager()
         if setproctitle:
             setproctitle(oldproctitle)
-        self.metric_queue = self.manager.Queue()
+        self.metric_queue = self.manager.Queue(512)

     def run(self):
         """
```

This would allow for transient handler failure and would limit the memory usage for that queue.

Then, what should be done in the `QueueHandler._flush()` method?  should it drop the current metric and log an error on `Queue.Full` exceptions?  Or should it block until there's some room available?

If it blocks, it would stop consuming resources until the handler recovers,  but then I think there's a `SIGALARM` shotgun that will kick in after a few seconds...

Any opinions on all this?

Here is an idea of having comprehensive test suite against our whole stack (repo/component integration test suite). We don't have org-level issued tracker so I'm placing it here for now.

There are 2 main reasons to do it:
- Different packages integration tests don't have a proper place to store. For example - integration between datapackage and sql/bigquery/* storage drivers.
- For now our test system is kinda fragile - it works on repo-level but we don't have kinda stack `checksum` to be sure everything works as expected. Example from real live - `datapackage-py` had an api breaking change -> all integrations stop to work but we even didn't know about it. The same could happen because of `tabulator-py` change etc.

So proposed way is to have `testsuite-py` repo triggered to run very high-level tests (it could be BDD for example) on every `tabulator-py`, `datapackage-py`, `jsontableschema-py`, `jsontableschema-*-py` etc push to master (or pypi):
- datasets from https://github.com/datasets + openspending dataset etc
- dataprotocols from http://dataprotocols.org/ (protocol change also could break stack)
- wrapped into `datapackage-py` or `jsontableschema-py`
- checked with `sql/bigquery/*` integrations - import/export

So having this we'll ensure our stack is working from top-to-bottom on every underlaying packages changes.

I've started this work as a part of `datapackage-storage-py` - https://github.com/okfn/datapackage-storage-py/tree/master/examples - but now it's going to be deprecated so I need a new place for this tests. And I think place it to `datapackage-py` will be wrong because it tests data + repo integrations (not related to datapackage).

When I try and delete the key, I see:

```
iblenke@ip-10-113-0-135 ~ $ deis login https://deis.mydomain.com
username: travis
password:
Logged in as travis
iblenke@ip-10-113-0-135 ~ $ deis keys
=== travis Keys
testing-te-worker-test1-373-linux-1 ssh-rsa AAAAB3Nz...y8vs3g9DnD
iblenke@ip-10-113-0-135 ~ $ deis keys help
Usage: deis keys:list
iblenke@ip-10-113-0-135 ~ $ deis keys:remove testing-te-worker-test1-373-linux-1
Removing testing-te-worker-test1-373-linux-1 SSH Key... 403 FORBIDDEN
Detail:
You do not have permission to perform this action.
```

So as an admin, I begrudgingly make this user an admin:

```shell
$ deis perms:create travis --admin
Adding travis to system administrators... done
```

And I can now delete this key as the user:

```shell
$ deis keys:remove testing-te-worker-test1-373-linux-1
Removing testing-te-worker-test1-373-linux-1 SSH Key... done
```

I'm going to bet we don't have this permissions problem again now that the user is an admin.

issue_title: How to interact with the storage layer?
issue_body: I was wondering if there are any docs on how to let applications write data to the redundant storage layer of deis?

issue_title: deisctl should warn when unit files have errors
issue_body:
We're using tags to isolate the control plane and application units to different hosts/autoscaling groups in EC2.

We ran into an issue earlier this week where a node failed and some of the deis control plane units moved to an unexpected node because the MachineMetadata option was incorrectly specified in some of the deis unit files ( MachineMetaData instead of MachineMetadata ).

Fleetctl will load the unit, but will give you a warning. It would be nice if deisctl did something similar or perhaps rejected the submission outright.

$ grep MachineMetaData deis-controller.service
MachineMetaData=role=services

$ fleetctl --version
fleetctl version 0.11.1

issue_title: provisioning should fail on a used discovery URL
issue_body:
A persistent problem is forgetting to run `make discovery-url` before provisioning a cluster. If it hasn't been run, and there's no user-data file, that is caught and handled well, but if a user-data exists with a discovery.etcd.io endpoint that's already been used, that isn't caught and provisioning will proceed and then fail with errors.

Potentially Deis could either:
- make an HTTP request of the discovery URL and fail if it exists, assuming that means it's been used already
- mark the user-data file with some canonical entry that will be caught and handled as an error on reuse, and/or make it invalid YAML
- generate a new disco URL on every provisioning (see #4548)

issue_title: Remove `evm.utils.padding` in favor of built-in ljust and rjust methods.
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The utility functions `pad_left` and `pad_right` are not necessary and are likely causing a performance hit.

### How can it be fixed

Replace all usage of them with calls to `str.ljust` `str.rjust`, `bytes.ljust`, `bytes.rjust`.

issue_title: Windows 10 - SSL Error only solved when installing python from python.org
issue_body:
Guys,
My set up: Win10 + miniconda with python 3.5.2 + PyCharm 2016.2.2
I have had strange issue when I was trying to use quandl package. The error was 
urllib.error.URLError: <urlopen error [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:645)>

code triggering this issue was : 
import Quandl as qd
ds = qd.get("FMAC/HPI_PA", authtoken="MyAuthTockenHere")
strange thing was that the same code was working on my old laptop on the same Windows 10 + miniconda and PyCharm installation. I've tried everything from Windows settings to miniconda and pycharm reinstallation along with all necessary packages update. Nothing worked only until I realised that on my old laptop I had Python 3.5.2 from python.org installed first before I moved to miniconda. So I installed Python 3.5 from Python.org and all started working immediately.

Fetching package metadata ......Could not connect to http://conda.binstar.org/myname/osx-64/
I tracked the error to the configuration file ~/.condarc
The default configuration file has
- http://conda.binstar.org/myname

issue_title: multiuser install - "text file is busy" when activating environment
issue_body:
Today, from a user account, I did

```
$ source activate anarel-1.0.0
```

and got

```
-bash: /central-install/miniconda2/bin/conda: /central-install/miniconda2/bin/python: bad interpreter: No such file or directory
```

I did it a second time and it worked.

we see this kind of thing on our filesystem when we do thinks like modify then install a file and immediately run the file from the new location, sleeping a second in between those steps fixes it. I think it is a problem with our nfs filesystem, but I was surprised to see it when I activate an environment. I know you guys are looking at multi-user conda, so wanted to raise the issue - to make conda robust on different filesystems, keep things like source activate as lightweight as possible? (Or tell us to fix our filesystem :)

issue_title: Conda is pulling the wrong `vc` when a package is unavailable in a preferred channel
issue_body:
@msarahan maybe you can help us here. As you can see from this log:

https://ci.appveyor.com/project/astrofrog/debug-conda-forge-appveyor/build/1.0.17#L82

even though that project is Python 2.7 and should be using `vc 9` conda is pulling a `qt` compiled with `vc 10`. Note that the project has `conda-forge` and `defaults` channels in that order and `conda-forge` does not have a `qt vc 9` version for that Arch. So conda should be pulling a `qt vc 9` from `defaults` instead.

If the channel order is resversed the right stack is downloaded.

IMO channel preference should not swicht `vc`s. I've seen a smiliar behavior when channel preference broke a package pinning in order to satisfy the channel order. Maybe `pinning`, like the `vc`s there, should preceed the channel order in the resolution in order to ensure a stable mix of packages.

issue_title: [Enhancement] make Offset Calculator work if called from code parts and navigation pane, too
issue_body:
Currently the offset calculator only works if used in definition parts of the code.
It would be useful to be able to use it in code parts, too. 

With the definition

``` ::cobol
       01  Output-Detail.
           05 OD-Addr.
              10 OD-Addr-Hex           OCCURS 8 TIMES PIC X.
           05 FILLER                   PIC X(1).
           05 OD-Byte                  PIC Z(3)9.
           05 FILLER                   PIC X(1).
           05 OD-Hex                   OCCURS 16 TIMES.
              10 OD-Hex-1              PIC X.
              10 OD-Hex-2              PIC X.
              10 FILLER                PIC X.
           05 OD-ASCII                 OCCURS 16 TIMES
                                       PIC X.
```

and the use of the calculator at "OD-Hex-1" (if possible even if the cursor is at "e")

``` ::cobol
               MOVE Hex-Digit (Left-Nibble)
                 TO OD-Hex-1 (Output-Sub)
```

the calculator could show the complete 01 level [or 77 if the variable itself is a lvl77 item] and have the line with OD-Hex-1 marked.

The same functionality would be nice if it would be available from the navigation pane (which has currently no context menu).

### Description

Steps to reproduce:
- open a source file
- open navigation pane if necessary
- collapse working-storace
- write code

result: all entries are uncollapsed again (even if navigation pane is "locked")
### System information
- Operating System: Windows
- OpenCobolIDE: 4.7.3.dev24
- GnuCOBOL: cobc (GNU Cobol) 1.1.0
- Python: 3.4.3 (32bits)
- Qt: 5.5.1
- PyQt: 5.5.1
- pyqode.core: 2.9.dev
- pyqode.cobol: 2.9.dev
- pyqode.qt: 2.9.dev
- pygments: 2.0.2
- QDarkStyle: 2.1

BTW: Seems like this either only happens on "big" source files or with source files where the missing header entries are assumed.

issue_title: [Bug] File system view is reloaded even when switching between files with same file path
issue_body:
I've opened two files from the same directory. When switching between them the "file system view" is refreshed. As the files are on a network share with lots of files in the directory this needs some time.

The good workaround is to lock the view.
The even better implementation would be to first get the "new" file path and checking if it differs from the file path of the current file system view - if not, there's no need to reload it at all.

Is there a version that supports elasticseach 5.0, or expected shortly?

issue_title: TransportError documentation not consistent with code for ConnectionError
issue_body:
The documention for TransportError says:
```
    @property
    def info(self):
        """ Dict of returned error info from ES, where available. """
        return self.args[2]
```

But ConnectionError, that is a subclass of TransportError:
```
   def __str__(self):
        return 'ConnectionError(%s) caused by: %s(%s)' % (
            self.error, self.info.__class__.__name__, self.info)
```

`self.info.__class__.__name__` is not very useful with a dict.

Support to delete multiple resources with dependent relationship
issue_body:
This is to support users can delete all resources created by `az vm/vmss create` so to avoid getting billed by leftovers such as the public IP. The flow by leveraging tags:
```bash
1.	az vm create -g myRG -n myVM --image centos --tag staging
2.	az resource delete –id $(az resource list –tag staging –query “[].id” -otsv)
```

When specifying Service Fabric entities, different commands use different parameters. For example some commands need a fabric name such as `fabric:/App` while others just need the id `App`.

These should be consistently either the name or the id.

Currently there's a hard coded check to only allow directory uploads. The image store now supports uploading zipped application packages. In order to speed up application uploads, consider using zipped uploads.

issue_title: PRI 0: Use the Offset parameter in append to ensure we are always appending at the offset we expect
issue_body:
The append API allows for a query parameter called offset, which allows you to specify where you think the end of the file is to append data to. This is very useful for us, since it allows us to submit an append call where we "think" the file should currently be. If data has already been uploaded at that location, you will receive a 400 error with a BadOffsetException, which you can catch. This will indicate that data is already at that location and we can move on to the next 4mb chunk to upload. This will help preserve the uploaded file and ensure that its final length is the same as the source file.

Here is a sample request/response where the offset is not the end of the file (meaning there is already data at or ahead of the offset specified):

**Request:** 
POST https://adlspysample01.azuredatalakestore.net/webhdfs/v1/sample.txt?op=APPEND&append=true&**offset=4**&api-version=2015-10-01-preview HTTP/1.1

issue_title: Add support for pcre / scripts / system hooks definition in .pre-commit-config.yaml
issue_body:
Everything is in the title :)

_Rationale:_ a `pre-commit` user shouldn't have to setup a git repository to configure a pre-commit check that can be defined in 5 lines or less.

_Example:_ taken the from [do_not_commit](https://github.com/pricematch/pricematch-pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/hooks.yaml) hook:

    - id: do_not_commit
      name: Block if "DO NOT COMMIT" is found
      entry: DO NOT COMMIT
      language: pcre
      files: ^(.*)$
 
_Suggested solutions:_

1. Allow for pcre / scripts / system hooks definition in _.pre-commit-config.yaml_
2. Allow for the `repo` field in _.pre-commit-config.yaml_ to point to a subfolder (of the git repo configured with `pre-commit`) that contains a _hooks.yaml_ .

```python
import time
import asyncio
import async_timeout
import aiohttp

async def fetch(session, url):
    t1 = time.time()
    async with async_timeout.timeout(10):

issue_title: Docs doesn't match code for version param in ClientSession.request()
issue_body:
## Long story short

Hi there. I noticed that in the aiohttp latest docs https://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/latest/client_reference.html#aiohttp.ClientSession.request mentions that a `version` param can be provided. But looking at the source code, `version` is set only during the session construction, but not at the session.request() method. So I'm wondering if it should be deleted from the docs or code has to be updated to allow version to be set also from the session.request() method, in addition to the session construction?

## Your environment

aiohttp master

## Long story short

Using part.set_content_disposition for some multipart file stuff, couldn't get it to work.
So I checked the part header, which is as I expected and then made a fake writer to check what the payload being sent actually was. The content disposition in the payload doesn't at all match what I have set in the part.

## Expected behaviour

The content disposition in the payload is as set in the part.

## Actual behaviour

It appears to generate a fresh content disposition.

## Steps to reproduce

![Demo](https://i.dabbitpri.me/qq42y4inzq.png)
```
class Writer:

    def __init__(self):
        self.buffer = ""

    async def write(self, stuff):
        self.buffer += stuff.decode('utf-8','ignore')

    def done(self):
        print(self.buffer[:300])

# Generate multipart
with aiohttp.MultipartWriter('form-data') as mp:
    part = mp.append(open(path, 'rb'))
    part.set_content_disposition(
        'form-data',
        quote_fields=False,
        name='files[]',
        filename=os.path.split(path)[1]
    )
    # Fix aiohttp being retarded
    part._headers['Content-Disposition'] = part._headers['Content-Disposition'].split(" filename*=utf-8", 1)[0]
    print("\n", part._headers['Content-Disposition'], "\n")
    w = Writer()
    await mp.write(w)
    w.done()
```

## Your environment

Aiohttp 3.2.1
Python 3.6
macOS 10.13.4

issue_title: Big chunk decoding makes content.iter_any() iterate over partial content
issue_body:
## Long story short

When receiving a very big chunk (response has `Transfer-Encoding: chunked.` header), result of `aiohttp.ClientSession().request(...).content.iter_any()` yields partial chunks.

## Expected behaviour

I would expect chunks integrity to be respected, and `iter_any()` to yield reasonably-decodable json content.

## Actual behaviour

`iter_any()` yields partial chunks, which is Bad(tm).

## Steps to reproduce

I think this basic code below is enough if you find a server that answers big chunks. We internally managed to reproduce the bug by querying a docker server with URL `GET /v1.24/containers/json?all=1 HTTP/1.1.`. Server would reply a very big 350 000 bytes long chunk, and instead of yielding that chunk iter_any() just served us an about-30k-bytes-long beginning of chunk.

```python
response = await aiohttp.ClientSession().request(...)
async for chunk in response.content.iter_any():
    json.loads(chunk)
```

If ValueError is raised, you lose the game.

## Your environment
Ubuntu Xenial (python 3.5.2) with aiohttp 2.0.7 (bug is reproducible on aiohttp master branch).

(update: updated code sample)

issue_title: Reduce amount of ssl related params in client API
issue_body:
Now we have `verify_ssl`, `fingerprint` and `ssl_context`.

The proposal is replacing all of them with `ssl`.
* if `ssl` is instance of `ssl.SSLContext` it is used as `ssl_context`
* if `ssl` is `False` -- `verify_ssl=False` logic is applied
* if `ssl` is instance of new `Fingerprint` class -- `fingerprit` check is applied

Existing params should be deprecated.

import warnings

x = 1 + 1

jupyter_core 4.3.0

issue_title: 'print' highlighting different in notebook vs editor
issue_body: Extremely small issue, but just noticed that in notebook if you type a print it gets bold highlighted (similar to: for, return, etc.) whereas in editor if you type a print it is unbold highlighted (similar to a python built-in function). Is there a reason it can't be the same on both? Or is it a setting I need to set.

issue_title: Enhancement: Option to allow serving from hidden directory
issue_body:
```
[I 21:48:37.098 NotebookApp] Refusing to serve hidden directory, via 404 Error
```
Since iCloud Drive folder on macOS is reachable from user folder but it goes through hidden folder, I would love to have the ability to override the hidden directory restriction so I can view it (if the jupyter server has user folder as root).

issue_title: Support Gif Mimetype & Image(format=) should overwrite autodetection of extension.
issue_body: When I update an gif on disk and recall IPython.image, the old version of the gif is displayed (inline). Is there a way to clear the cache explicitly?

issue_title: Wrong size for some interface buttons in the tree view with Firefox
issue_body:
![screen](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8852116/32812029-3abf8af2-c9a3-11e7-9c77-e5785d35f42f.png)

Using Firefox, there are a lot of small sizing problem across the whole UI.
The ones highlighted in the screenshot above are the among the most annoying since they appear often in the daily usage.

I'll send a pull request later on to fix them :)

issue_title: How to disable notebook mobile version? I wanna use destop version only on my server.
issue_body: How to disable notebook mobile version? I want to use destop version only on my phone and pad.

issue_title: ListType(choices=) prevents empty lists from being valid
issue_body:
When adding `choices=` to `ListType` validation becomes muddied because an empty list is no longer valid. Even when `required=False` there seems to be no way to have choices and also be fine with an empty list.

Could this key off of required? Or perhaps even a new config option such as `allow_empty=bool`?

Often times, JSON responses send a large blob of information, while applications only care about a few of the fields. For instance, a Person API might send 'full_name', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'age' 'height', etc. However if my application only cares about the person's first name, and age, then I'd like to define a model in my app such as

```python
class Person(Model):
    first_name = StringType()
    age = IntType()
```

This only has the fields that I care about. In this case, if the Person API sends me extra fields, or changes its responses to add more fields, I'd like to ignore them since my application doesn't care about them. However with the current implementation of schematics, all extraneous ('Rogue') fields throw a schematics.exceptions.ModelConversionError. So I have to define all the extra fields in the model, even tho I never use them.

Is there a way of telling a model to ignore 'Rogue' fields? If not, I was thinking I could define a ModelOption that basically suppresses any Rogue Field exceptions for extraneous fields encountered in the response.

issue_title: Cannot find FANN source libs in mutliarch
issue_body:
When trying to install this library I get: 
$ pip install .
Processing /home/johndoe/src/fann2
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Looking for FANN libs...
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "", line 1, in 
      File "/tmp/pip-e23ii2o4-build/setup.py", line 92, in 
        build_swig()
      File "/tmp/pip-e23ii2o4-build/setup.py", line 85, in build_swig
        find_fann()
      File "/tmp/pip-e23ii2o4-build/setup.py", line 73, in find_fann
        raise Exception("Couldn't find FANN source libs!")
    Exception: Couldn't find FANN source libs!
    
    ----------------------------------------
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-e23ii2o4-build/
The reason is that on multiarch systems (at least Debian-based ones) libdoublefann is located in `/usr/lib/$ARCH/libdoublefann.so` (eg. `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libdoublefann.so`):: Patching the setup code like this fixes the issue: 
def find_fann():
    '''Find doublefann library'''
    # FANN possible libs directories (as $LD_LIBRARY_PATH), also includes
    # pkgsrc framework support.
    if sys.platform == "win32":
        dirs = sys.path
        for ver in dirs:
            if os.path.isdir(ver):
                if find_x(ver):
                    return True
        raise Exception("Couldn't find FANN source libs!")
    else:
        # Fixed the path here:
        dirs = ['/lib', '/usr/lib/', '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu', '/usr/lib64', '/usr/local/lib', '/usr/pkg/lib']
        for path in dirs:
            if os.path.isdir(path):
                if find_x(path):
                    return True
        raise Exception("Couldn't find FANN source libs!")

issue_title: Catch KeyErrors from data_ref attributes in lint and test mode
issue_body:
`planemo` is not able to test and lint `data_ref` attributes like `type="data_column" data_ref="inputA"`. Wrong values will result in a KeyError which is not recognised by `lint`. `test` will not run any test which results in `OK`.

More informations here:
https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/148
planemo.readthedocs.org is redirecting to planemo.readthedocs.io which doesn't exist or can't be found (as of 31/10/2016 6:03 UTC)

@mr-c "@jmchilton a GitHub repo and an adaptation of the following:
```
whoami
cloneorpull() {
        if test -d $1 ; then
                (cd $1 && git pull)
        else
                git clone $2
        fi
}
venv() {
        if ! test -d $1 ; then
                virtualenv $1
        fi
        . $1/bin/activate
}
cloneorpull common-workflow-language https://github.com/common-workflow-language/common-workflow-language.git
venv cwltool-venv
docker pull node:slim
(. cwltool-venv/bin/activate && python setup.py install)
(. cwltool-venv/bin/activate && cd common-workflow-language && ./run_test.sh RUNNER=cwltool)
```
Ideally just a change to the last line's RUNNER=..."

* it's 1000 lines of code
* It contains multiple functions that are over 50 lines long.
* It can contains a class (that should be able to simplify a few things) but in this class multiple methods are over 50 lines long.
* A global log variable is defined
* It overwrites the ConnectionError that is in standard library.
* It has by far the highest complexity of all files in ephemeris (18)

Shed-tools is not pleasant to work at. Navigating through it is a jungle. It should be totally refactored.
I have a few suggestions how to approach this:
* Take `get_tool_list_from_galaxy.py` as an example. This was done for `run-data-managers` as well, and as a result complexity was dropped, while at the same time increasing functionality.
Essentially this means shed_tools should be written as a class that:
    - Has a method to output currently installed tools
    - Has a method to check whether a tool is currently installed
    - Has a method to install a tool
    - Has a method to test a tool (Thanks a lot @jmchilton !)
    - Has some sort of install_tools method for multiple tools including logging.
* The parsing of the tool list yaml may be moved to a separate file
* Some stuff from get_tool_list from galaxy might be reused in shed-tools. (Like determining current tool list)
* Some redundant command line options should be removed. Installing by passing a yaml string? OK. Installing by tool_list file? OK. Installing by specifying every frigging tool attribute on the commandline? We can drop that IMO.

This is something I have been thinking about doing it for a while. Just putting it out there before we increase the complexity of shed-tools even further. I think refactoring will also improve the ability to test using pytest #80.

This is currently living in lib/galaxy/datatypes/util.
I think that would be another candidate that we could put in here, what do you think?
AFAIK it's only use on the tool side is in cufflinks, and that's really just two small functions, which could also be copied into the tool itself.

I have a workflow where I develop a 'guest' project that lives in a subfolder of a 'host' project. When I want to make changes in the guest, it is much easier and straightforward to not do them in the guest's original repository but right in the subfolder within the host; that way, a test run immediately shows me whether my updates work. I then use a diff/merge tool (`meld`) to merge all changes back into the guest's repository, from where I commit and push to remote. 

At that moment in time, the contentent of the referenced guest repo and the contents of the guest subfolder are 100% identical. Sadly, when I then do `peru reup`, `peru` will complain that 'imported files have been modified', which is sort of true and sort of false. It's true that I did touch those files, but their contents are bit-identical with the ones that peru will only import with `--force`. IMHO this is questionable behavior; if I want to do `cp A B` but avoid to overwrite changes, then, clearly, `sha1sum A` equals `sha1sum B` I can totally do or not do `cp A B`, the only difference being possibly the file dates on `B` that get updated on copy. At any rate, it should cause an opt-out as no content changes will be effected either way.

My suggestion is to first check both the new and the old files' `sha1sum`s, and never opt out when both are identical.

issue_title: Option to get dependency as a full git clone
issue_body:
Just found Peru and think it looks very promising.

Although, I have one feature suggestion that I believe would make it even more useful.

If the directory that you get the dependency code in would in fact be a git clone. That way, if you as part of your project changes anything to the the imported module you can easily commit it and send it back upstream.

As it is now you must copy the changed files to another location where you have a clone already and it makes it a bit harder to work with.

It's fine if the clone checkout the sha1 commit as a detached head. Just keep the git info there to use if you need to :)

What do you think, is this desired? Is it possible to do?

issue_title: How to disable the full game rendering in windows
issue_body: Hi, I want to know that if I can disable the full game rendering in wondows, because the  rendering takes up a lot of resources.  It seems that we should pass some parameters to SC2_x64.exe, so I wonder whether there is a parameter to disable it? :D

In the SC2 LE paper there's this sentence under input pre-processing:
> We embed all feature layers containing categorical values into a continuous space which is
equivalent to using a one-hot encoding in the channel dimension followed by a 1 × 1 convolution.

This raises two question on implementation detail. Let's assume we're dealing with a `64x64` minimap and we want to embed `visibility_map` (4 levels) and `player_relative` (5 levels) features.

1.) What Is embedding dimension? That is, what is the number of kernels used in 1x1 conv?
i.e. if it's `1` then our final (concatenated) output dimensions would be `64x64x2`.

2.) Is embedding done separately per each feature or with one pass-through for all? More specifically:
 * 1) one-hot on channel -> concat on channel -> 1x1 conv on all features at the same time.  
ex. one-hot to `64x64x4` and `64x64x5` -> concat to `64x64x9` -> 1x1(x2) conv to `64x64x2` output
 * 2) one-hot on channel -> 1x1 conv separately per feature -> concat on channel
ex. one-hot to `64x64x4` and `64x64x5` -> 1x1(x1) conv to `64x64x1` and `64x64x1` -> concat to `64x64x2` output

The big difference between the two is that in first case all features influence output channels at the same time.

issue_title: charms.reactive.relations assumes the relation name is a key
issue_body:
The RelationBase and Conversation model assumes that a relation name name is a key, rather than using the relation id + unit name. This will cause a reactive charm to fail, depending how the related service chooses to use it. For example, here is a metadata.yaml snippet from a client that stores its data in three separate silos:

```yaml
requires:
    session:
        interface: pgsql
    public:
        interface: pgsql
    confidential:
        interface: pgsql
```

On the client side, everything is fine. However, on the server side we have this:

```yaml
provides:
    db:
        interface: pgsql
```

If the client is related three times to the same server, which would be common for development and staging, then the reactive model fails as the relation name can not be used as a key to identify which of the three db interfaces is needed. The relation id must be used.
There are more references to requirements.txt than test-requirements.txt in our source code and in other systems we both currently use and could potentially use.
See #200. We punted on this in a rush to get v0.9.0 tagged.

Need to clean up checkpointing behaviour of standalone tools. 
* ex toil-vg-index writes the index to the job store in addition to the out_store

Option to turn off checkpoing
* ex right now gam always written to store.  Allow users to turn this off

Review current strategy to copy when checkpointing.  
* For instance, when checkpointing the gam, we copy it to the out_store in addition to the job store.  Is it smarter just to leave it in the out_store only?  This is somewhat dependent on toil implementation and environment.
Once RNA-seq repo has pushed a stable release to PyPi.

As of https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil/commit/6456fe81b385ac9314ec684be9fd5d41fb8d545a, the Spark support in toil-scripts is duplicative and should be removed in favor of the upstream Spark service code.

issue_title: Create a script to generate an overview of the supported formats
issue_body:
* [ ] Create a script to generate an overview of the supported formats

Also see: https://github.com/log2timeline/plaso/issues/279

It would be great to have a pytest plugin in order to run it as part of the unittests

`py.test --pep8-naming`

We're using flake8 with namespace packages and we'd like to group our imports like this:

```python
import functools

import google.auth  # separate package
from google.cloud import core  # separate package
import six

from google.cloud.bigquery import table  # package-local, we're in google/cloud/bigquery/job.py
```

It's fine if we need to specify this via a flag (like `--application-import-names=google.cloud.bigquery`)

Presently we must either choose between putting all `google.*` imports in the 2nd grouping or the 3rd grouping.

issue_title: Error: warnings has no attribute DeprecationWarning
issue_body:
While testing `pluggy` off the branch from #72, I came across this error:

```
        if '__multicall__' in hookimpl.argnames:
            warnings.warn(
                "Support for __multicall__ is now deprecated and will be"
                "removed in an upcoming release.",
>               warnings.DeprecationWarning
            )
E           AttributeError: module 'warnings' has no attribute 'DeprecationWarning'
```


https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/557cd3f0245347c1cd359c6deb4b25cde944e98a/pluggy/__init__.py#L656-L661

And indeed `warnings` module doesn't have a `DeprecationWarning`.

We should add a test to catch this warnings (and any others we might have).
